params.name: Grignard Reagent
params.description:
On Apr 2, 1901 AD, the French chemist Grignard formulated the Grignard Reagent during the experiment.
Grignard Reagent was discovered by a French chemist named Grignard. It is an organometallic compound containing magnesium halide, a kind of nucleophile, as it contains carbanion. Grignard reagents are for various reactions such as coupling, addition, and substitution and have considerable application value in the organic synthesis.
